SDG 17

Partnerships are central to KHAS’s sustainability strategy. The University collaborates with international networks, civil society organizations, municipalities, and EU-funded projects such as Erasmus+ to advance the Sustainable Development Goals. Through initiatives like GenderEX, Boundary Crossing, and social responsibility projects with TİDER and local NGOs, KHAS leverages cross-sector collaboration to enhance social impact. By connecting research, education, and community engagement, the University strengthens its role as a hub for sustainable development partnerships at the local, national, and global levels.

Faculty: Faculty of Art and Design | Unit / Center: Turkish Theatre Foundation & Kadir Has University | Activity Type: Panel | Activity Title: “Conversations on History in Our Theatre XXIII – Zeynep Günsür Yüceil” | Activity Date: 15 May 2024 | Summary: The 23rd session of the “Conversations on History in Our Theatre” series was held at Kadir Has University with Zeynep Günsür Yüceil’s talk titled “On the TAL (Theatre Research Laboratory) Archive.” During the talk, Yüceil highlighted key aspects of this rich and long-standing research project based on written and visual records from the TAL archive and opened a discussion on how it could serve as a resource for future research.

[12]

Faculty: Faculty of Art and Design | Unit / Center: Turkish Theatre Foundation & Kadir Has University | Activity Type: Panel | Activity Title: “Conversations on History in Our Theatre XXIV – Özlem Hemi̇ş” | Activity Date: 10 June 2024 | Summary: The 24th session of the “Conversations on History in Our Theatre” series took place at Kadir Has University with Özlem Hemi̇ş’s talk, “Fragile Male Portraits in Turkish Theatre.” The session explored the issue of “fragile masculinity,” defined as men’s fear of losing their socially constructed status shaped by entrenched masculinity norms, through theatre characters reflecting these symptoms.

[13]

Faculty: Faculty of Art and Design | Unit / Center: Beykoz Kundura | Activity Type: Panel | Activity Title: Özlem Hemi̇ş | Activity Date: 16 December 2023 | Summary: “Local Content, New Form,” curated and moderated by Özlem Hemi̇ş and Ayşe Draz, featured Prof. Dr. Dikmen Gürün, who offered a perspective on the shaping of Turkey’s theatre scene and cultural policies in the country. The discussion included BK’s educational programs and the works supported as a producer, in dialogue with the artists.

Faculty: Faculty of Art and Design | Unit / Center: Theatre | Educational Activity Name: Zorlu PSM Workshop | Date: 10 January – 31 May 2023 | Participants: Trainers: Serdar Biliş, Özlem Hemi̇ş, Özlem Özhabes; Trainees: aspiring playwrights, directors, and producers who applied for scholarships. | Summary: PSM Workshop is a free training program by Zorlu PSM designed to bring new talents to theatre, enrich original content, and contribute to the sustainability of theatre with expert trainers.

8 December 2023 – Within the collaboration with Abdi İbrahim, students received a brief for the Lornox Fast drug promotion campaign. Competing against professional agencies, one of our student teams won the competition.

8 March 2023 – Advertising Department students visited Rafineri Agency upon the invitation of Strategy Director Can Çalışkan.

5 December 2024 – Department Chair Assoc. Prof. Perrin Öğün Emre and Workshop Coordinator Ali Doğan met with Tribal Istanbul Agency President Arda Erdik to discuss potential future collaborations.

12 December 2024 – Assoc. Prof. Perrin Öğün Emre and Ali Doğan visited Pixiu Digital Agency, founded by alumni Can Akalın and Fatih Dilekçi, to discuss potential collaborations.

19 December 2024 – Department Chair Assoc. Prof. Perrin Öğün Emre and Workshop Coordinator Ali Doğan met with Serdesin Agency Founder Serhat Bayram to evaluate cooperation opportunities.

10 December 2024 – Havas Health & You + Buzz Health Agency General Manager Selim Tanören and Creative Director Medeni Yiğit presented their award-winning “Kontrol Sende, Cevabı Gende” campaign to our students and shared a new brief.

17.2. Projects

Interlibrary Cooperation (2023):

Collaboration with national and international networks such as ANKOS KİTS, ULAKBİM-TÜBES, and OCLC.

Institutional Partnerships (2023):

Income generated from the award-winning Green Library Project poster was donated to the KHAS White Doves Scholarship Fund.

Community-Service Projects:

Support provided for student clubs’ social responsibility initiatives.

Interlibrary Cooperation (2024):

Continued collaboration with ANKOS KİTS, ULAKBİM-TÜBES, and OCLC WorldShare.

Publisher Partnerships:

Continued Read-and-Publish agreements with publishers through ULAKBİM-EKUAL and the Anadolu University Libraries Consortium.

Institutional Partnerships (Cultural Heritage):

The “Reşat Ekrem Koçu Istanbul Encyclopaedia” web project was conducted in partnership with Garanti BBVA-SALT and Kadir Has University.

KHAS110 Social Responsibility Project: Maintains a stakeholder list of NGOs and university clubs. Students select an organization (focused on animals, environment, children, urban participation, food waste, etc.) and conduct team-based fieldwork, gaining first-hand experience in active citizenship and NGO operations. Partners in 2024–25 include IMM Women & Family Directorate, Istanbul City Council, Tider, Şişli & Beyoğlu Social Services Directorates, All Children Are Ours Association, Digital Knowledge Association, Four-Legged City Association, and Hrant Dink Foundation.

Artistic Research Project | “ACT – Artistic Freedom and Children’s and Youth’s Perspective in the Arts in Turkey”

Project Lead: Lecturer Özlem Özhabes

Researchers: Assoc. Assoc. Prof. Dr. Zeynep Günsür Yüceil, Asst. Prof. Dr. Özlem Hemiş

Duration: 01 June 2022 – 31 May 2024

Funded by the Swedish government and initiated by KHAS Theatre Department, ACT is a two-year research and education project focused on children’s and youth theatre. It is conducted in collaboration with Unga Klara – Sweden’s National Theatre for Children and Youth, Mordem Art (Diyarbakır), and K2 Contemporary Art Association (Izmir), aiming to train future artistic producers in this field.

[18]

Artistic Education Research Project | “Boundary Crossing – Artistic Research in Context”

Duration: 20 March 2023 – 12 May 2023

Partners: HKU University of the Arts Utrecht, Glej Theatre (Ljubljana), KHAS Theatre Department (Istanbul)

This pilot project promotes interdisciplinary artistic production, encouraging young artists to expand their creative tools, engage in collaborative research across disciplines, and explore new areas of thinking. Emphasis is placed on collective exploration and co-creation.

Artistic Education Research Project | “Boundary Crossing – Artistic Research in Context”

Duration: 23 September 2024 – 18 October 2024

Partners: Glej Theatre (Ljubljana, Slovenia), HKU University of the Arts Utrecht, KHAS Theatre Department (Istanbul)

Building on the pilot launched in 2021, the project continues as an experimental educational program connecting young theatre makers from the participating countries into 2025. “Boundary Crossing” explores collaboration across national and artistic borders, as well as individual thinking processes and working methods. The methodology focuses on developing theatre-making practices through engagement with multiple contexts, perspectives, cultures, and roles.

Participatory Governance in YDYO (School of Foreign Languages): Regular communication channels have been established between faculty management and student representatives. Bi-weekly meetings collect structured feedback, identify issues, and develop solutions. This dialogue promotes participatory governance and strengthens solidarity within the university community.

Regular Communication Between Faculty Administration and Student Representatives (SFL): Structured communication channels have been established between the School of Foreign Languages (SFL) administration and student representatives. Bi-weekly meetings are held to collect regular feedback through representation, identify issues, and develop solution proposals. This dialogue environment supports participatory governance and strengthens solidarity within the university community.

17.4. Courses

Faculty: Graduate School – Department of Art and Design / Cultural Heritage Conservation MA Program (Thesis / Non-Thesis) | Course: KK502 Legal Aspects and Practices of Conservation | Learning Outcome: Provides advanced knowledge and understanding of international and national legal regulations and organizational structures related to conservation and develops the ability to engage in critical discussions.

Faculty: Graduate School – Department of Art and Design / Cultural Heritage Conservation MA Program (Thesis / Non-Thesis) | Course: KK501 Concepts of Conservation | Learning Outcome: Provides advanced knowledge and comprehension of the historical evolution of conservation culture, conceptual foundations, conservation theories, national and international legal frameworks, social responsibility in conservation, and ethical values, while fostering the ability to engage in conceptual discussions.
